_notes.purpose: Template for creating new fraud benchmark scenarios.
_notes.instructions: Copy this file, fill in the fields, validate with scripts/validate_benchmark_dataset.py, and add it to datasets/fraud_benchmarks.json.

title: A descriptive title for the benchmark scenario
input_prompt: Analyze this month's payments for ghost vendor risk.

top_drivers.description: A payment was issued to a vendor with unverified registration status.

_field_notes.scenario_id: Unique identifier in lowercase snake_case (e.g. ghost_vendor_detection). Must match format ^[a-z0-9_]+$.
_field_notes.title: Short descriptive title for reporting and UI.
_field_notes.category: The business category, e.g. accounts_payable, vendor_management, payroll, accounting.
_field_notes.risk_type: The risk classification, e.g. duplicate_invoice, ghost_vendor, conflict_of_interest, shell_entity.
_field_notes.severity: The ground truth risk severity level. One of: info, low, medium, high, critical.
_field_notes.tags: A non-empty list of tags for filtering benchmarks, e.g. ['vendor', 'payments'].
_field_notes.input_prompt: The simulated user question or instruction to the agent.
_field_notes.expected_findings: A non-empty list of lowercase phrases that the agent must mention in its findings text.
_field_notes.expected_severity: The expected severity level classified by the agent (should match severity).
_field_notes.expected_evidence_patterns: A non-empty list of structural patterns the agent must include in its findings evidence. Each pattern can specify 'type', 'min_count', or 'id_contains'.
_field_notes.expected_recommended_action: The recommended next action the agent should propose (e.g. review_findings).
_field_notes.false_positive_guardrails: A non-empty list of keyword phrases characteristic of other fraud scenarios that must NOT be mentioned. If mentioned, it counts as a false positive.
